name: Coleman Hawkins
abstract: Coleman Randolph Hawkins (November 21, 1904 – May 19, 1969), nicknamed "Hawk" and sometimes "Bean", was an American jazz tenor saxophonist. One of the first prominent jazz musicians on his instrument, as Joachim E.

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: He’d play Louis or play Coleman Hawkins and Johnny Hodges. ... when you listen to Coleman Hawkins play a melody... I think I got that – began to get some of this from Pres in the beginning... Coleman Hawkins and Johnny Hodges, Ben Webster even. Don Byas as well. They play on pitch...
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Derek Brown
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee only responds to the interviewer's quote about Coleman Hawkins with 'Yeah. I can resonate with that.' There is no indication of a personal meeting, collaboration, or mentorship.
about_quotes.doc_title: Derek Brown interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 5, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Ramsey Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: The transcript mentions 'Bean' only as a nickname for Edwina Holmes ('we call her Bean, but Edwina'). There is no mention of Coleman Hawkins (also known as Bean) in the text.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ramsey Lewis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Slam Stewart
about_quotes.evidence: I did a gig with Coleman Hawkins in Providence, Rhode Island... I was still with Tatum, but I did this one week with Coleman Hawkins and Roy in this club in Providence.

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: You listen to the sound between a Coleman Hawkins and a Lester Young... Saxophone players — from Chu Berry to Lester Young to Coleman Hawkins to Ben Webster...
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Watts
about_quotes.evidence: Like Coleman Hawkins, he had to use a mic with that tenor, because he was soft. But when you let him get by that mic, man, he was just like Coleman Hawkins.
